text_blocks.text: differentiated effector cells, such as cytotoxic
natural killer cells and CTLs (cytotoxic T
lymphocytes), peak in order to elicit an effective
response against any intruding pathogens. As well
molecules, such as cortisol and catecholamines, peak.
There are two theories as to why the
pro-inflammatory state is reserved for sleep time.
First, inflammation would cause serious cognitive and
physical impairments if it were to occur during
wake times. Second, inflammation may occur during
sleep times due to the presence of melatonin.
Inflammation causes a great deal of oxidative stress
and the presence of melatonin during sleep times
could actively counteract free radical production
